name: Cumberland Municipal Employees
description_short: Cumberland Municipal Employees Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving municipal employees and families in Cumberland, RI with competitive lending rates and federally insured savings accounts.
description_long:
Cumberland Municipal Employees Federal Credit Union was established in 1962 as the Cumberland School Employees Credit Union and rebranded in 1993 to serve a broader municipal workforce. The credit union operates as a non-profit institution owned and governed by its members, with a Board of Directors and Supervisory Committee elected from the membership. It currently serves 1,163 members with $7.5 million in assets and is headquartered at 2145 Diamond Hill Road in Cumberland, Rhode Island. The institution is federally insured by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), protecting member deposits up to $250,000.

CMEFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including share accounts, auto loans (new and used), personal loans, secured loans, Certificates of Deposit (CDs), motorcycle loans, holiday loans, and savings clubs (Christmas, Vacation, and Miscellaneous). Members can also access free notary public services and set up automatic payroll deductions for savings. Current rates include auto loans at 5.75% APR for 2025-2026 models and 6-month CDs at 3.05% APR with a $1,000 minimum. The credit union emphasizes personalized service and community focus, with staff available during regular business hours (Monday-Friday 9am-4pm, with adjusted summer hours).

Unlike large commercial banks, CMEFCU maintains a membership-focused structure with lower-than-market rates on loans and competitive CD rates. The institution prioritizes relationship-based lending, encouraging members to call directly for loan applications rather than requiring complex online processes. The credit union limits membership to employees of specific municipal employers—the Town of Cumberland, Cumberland School Department, Police Department, Fire Department, EMS, Housing Authority, and Northern Rhode Island Collaborative—creating a tightly defined member base with shared interests.

The credit union is well-suited for eligible municipal employees seeking personalized service and competitive rates on auto and personal loans. However, prospective members should verify employment eligibility before opening an account, and the limited asset base and small membership may result in less robust technology infrastructure compared to larger financial institutions. The 9am-4pm weekday-only operating hours may pose accessibility challenges for some workers.
services: Share accounts (membership savings accounts with $25 minimum)
Auto loans for new and used vehicles with rates from 5.75%-6.95% APR
Secured loans at savings rate plus 3.50%
Certificates of Deposit (6-month and 12-month terms at 3.05% APR)
Payroll deduction setup for automatic transfers to share accounts
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 5.75% APR for 2025-2026 models with up to 84-month terms
Federally insured deposits up to $250,000 backed by NCUA and U.S. Government
Free notary public services available to members during business hours
Member-owned non-profit structure ensuring returns benefit members rather than shareholders
Multiple savings club options (Christmas, Vacation, Miscellaneous) with automatic distribution dates
Flexible auto financing including 100% financing available on qualifying vehicle years
Simple loan application process—direct phone contact eliminates complex online procedures
cons: Membership restricted to employees of specific municipal employers—not open to general public
Limited operating hours (Monday-Friday 9am-4pm, reduced to 3pm-12pm in summer) restrict access for many workers
Small institutional size ($7.5M in assets) may limit product offerings and technology capabilities compared to larger credit unions
Personal loan APR of 12% is significantly higher than auto loans, indicating limited personal lending competitiveness
No mention of online banking, mobile app, or digital services on website
best_for: Town of Cumberland municipal employees seeking competitive auto financing and personalized service
Cumberland School Department, Police, Fire, and EMS staff wanting member-owned financial institution benefits
Employees of Northern Rhode Island Collaborative seeking safe, federally insured savings and loan products
Municipal workers prioritizing relationship-based lending over digital-only banking experiences
diagnosis: Cumberland Municipal Employees Federal Credit Union is best for eligible municipal employees in Cumberland, Rhode Island who value personalized, relationship-based lending and member-owned institutional benefits. The critical caveat is strict membership eligibility—you must work for one of the specific municipal employers served (Town of Cumberland, School Department, Police/Fire/EMS, Housing Authority, or Northern Rhode Island Collaborative) to join. Additionally, limited operating hours and lack of visible digital banking services may frustrate members seeking 24/7 access or modern fintech features.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
